description.value:
Type: Ninjutsu

Rank: 3

Chakra Cost: 4

Learn DC: 15 | Successes: 2

Perform: 6 | Perform DC: 16

Components: C, F, Mas

The main weakness of a puppeteer is the fact that even controlling powerful weapons, his body is fragile and defenceless when attacked directly. To counter this, some shinobi learned how to use their puppets on a defensive maneuverer. This technique can only be used if there is at least one puppet currently controlled by the user by means of techniques such as Kugutsu no Jutsu or Akahigi within range. The targeted puppet is moved between the defended creature (That also must be in range) and the incoming attack. Once per round as an instant action, the user may perform this technique to aid himself or an ally to defend against an incoming attack; doing so provokes an attack of opportunity, unless the technique is performed defensively. The Perform DC in this case is equal to the attack roll or the Save DC plus 10 of the incoming attack. Failure by more than 10 means that the technique is still performed and costs half its normal cost. The chakra cost of the technique can't be converted, cannot be used against opponents of ECL higher than 1 and can't be used if the target is flat-footed or otherwise denied his dexterity bonus to Defence.

If the attack roll would be enough to hit the Defence of the puppet used on this technique (consider it flatfooted), the puppet takes full damage from the attack, otherwise is not damaged. Attacks that require saving throws (such as single target techniques or ray attacks) always cause full effect to the puppet, regardless of the attack roll. This technique can be used against, but won't defend from area of effect attacks, unless the puppet has the energy shield component, on which case the technique can be used to defend against such attacks. The puppet still takes full damage.

At the end of this technique, the puppet used to defend is on a random square within 5ft of the user (Usually directly in front of him).

Requires: Kugutsu no Jutsu (5), and Advanced Puppetry I (a)

Mastery: Every step of mastery increases the maximum ECL to the nearest unit of 5. The cost to perform the technique increases by 2 every step (6 for ECL 1-5, 8 for ECL 6-10, 10 for ECL 11-15, 12 for ECL 16-20 and 14 for ECL 21-25.)
